articles.headline: RBI board approves record Rs 2.11 lakh crore surplus transfer to government
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India board approved a record surplus transfer of Rs 2,10,874 crore to the central government for the 2023-24 fiscal year. This dividend is significantly higher than the budgeted estimate of Rs 1.02 lakh crore, providing a major fiscal boost. The decision was taken during a board meeting in Mumbai chaired by Governor Shaktikanta Das.

articles.headline: Sensex and Nifty hit record highs after RBI dividend announcement
articles.summary: Benchmark indices Sensex and Nifty surged to record intraday highs following the RBI's dividend announcement. The Sensex jumped nearly 1,200 points to cross the 75,000 mark. Investors reacted positively to the improved fiscal space for the government, which could lead to lower borrowing and controlled deficits. Banking stocks led the gains.

articles.headline: IndiGo parent InterGlobe Aviation reports record annual profit of Rs 8,172 crore
articles.summary: InterGlobe Aviation, parent company of IndiGo, reported a record net profit of Rs 8,172 crore for the full fiscal year 2024. For the fourth quarter, profit rose 106% to Rs 1,895 crore. Despite challenges with grounded aircraft, the airline benefited from strong passenger demand, increased capacity, and higher yields during the peak travel season.

articles.headline: Election Commission increases surveillance for Phase 6 of general elections
articles.summary: The Election Commission of India has directed officials to intensify monitoring for the sixth phase of polling. Enforcement agencies are tasked with curbing the flow of illicit cash, liquor, and freebies. The commission also addressed several complaints regarding Model Code of Conduct violations by high-profile leaders during recent election rallies.

articles.headline: NHAI plans to award highway projects worth Rs 1 lakh crore in FY25
articles.summary: The National Highways Authority of India aims to award 3,000 km of highway projects worth Rs 1 lakh crore in the 2024-25 fiscal year. The authority is shifting focus toward the Build-Operate-Transfer (BOT) model to attract more private investment. This strategy is intended to reduce the government's capital expenditure while maintaining infrastructure growth.

articles.headline: India turns net importer of steel in FY24 as imports jump 38%
articles.summary: India became a net importer of steel in the 2023-24 fiscal year after a gap of several years. Imports surged 38% to 8.3 million tonnes, while exports grew marginally to 7.5 million tonnes. Domestic demand remained robust, but competitive pricing from China and South Korea led to higher inflows of finished steel products into the Indian market.

articles.headline: UK annual inflation falls to 2.3 percent hitting three year low
articles.summary: Annual inflation in the United Kingdom fell sharply to 2.3% in April, down from 3.2% in March. This is the lowest level since July 2021 and brings inflation close to the Bank of England's 2% target. Lower domestic energy bills were the primary driver of the drop, though analysts suggest interest rate cuts may still be delayed due to high services inflation.

articles.headline: Microsoft launches Copilot+ PCs featuring advanced on-device artificial intelligence tools
articles.summary: Microsoft has introduced Copilot+ PCs, a new category of personal computers with integrated AI hardware. These devices utilize neural processing units (NPUs) to run AI tasks directly on the device. Key features include 'Recall', which logs user activity for easy retrieval, and real-time live captions. The move aims to integrate generative AI into daily computing.

articles.headline: AstraZeneca immunotherapy drug Imfinzi improves survival in small cell lung cancer
articles.summary: AstraZeneca announced that its drug Imfinzi significantly extended life for patients with limited-stage small cell lung cancer in a Phase 3 trial. The treatment reduced the risk of death by 27% compared to the placebo group. This is the first immunotherapy to show a survival benefit in this setting, potentially changing the standard of care for patients.

articles.headline: Heatwave conditions persist across North India with temperatures reaching 47 degrees
articles.summary: Severe heatwave conditions continued to sweep through North and Northwest India, with several stations recording temperatures above 47 degrees Celsius. Delhi and parts of Rajasthan were the worst hit. The power demand in the national capital reached an all-time peak of 7,717 MW. Authorities have issued health advisories to prevent heat-related illnesses.

articles.headline: Adani Power gets NCLT approval to acquire Lanco Amarkantak Power
articles.summary: The National Company Law Tribunal has approved Adani Power’s resolution plan to acquire Lanco Amarkantak Power for Rs 4,101 crore. This acquisition will expand Adani Power’s capacity in the thermal power sector. Lanco Amarkantak operates a 1,200 MW thermal power plant in Chhattisgarh. The acquisition is expected to be completed within the current fiscal year.

articles.headline: Solar power installations in India hit record 10GW in first quarter
articles.summary: India added a record 10 gigawatts of solar capacity in the first quarter of 2024, a 400% increase compared to the previous year. According to a report by Mercom India, the surge was driven by several delayed projects becoming operational. Rajasthan and Gujarat led the country in new utility-scale solar installations during this period.

articles.headline: ISRO successfully conducts landing mission for Pushpak reusable launch vehicle
articles.summary: The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) completed the third landing mission for its Reusable Launch Vehicle 'Pushpak' in Karnataka. The mission demonstrated the vehicle's autonomous landing capability under challenging conditions. This success brings India closer to developing low-cost access to space through reusable technology.

articles.headline: Four dead as ethnic violence flares up again in Manipur
articles.summary: Manipur's ethnic conflict claimed four lives on Wednesday. Three Thadou-Kuki church leaders were killed in an ambush in Kangpokpi while returning from a conference, and a Chiru-Naga civilian was shot dead in Noney district. The killings triggered indefinite shutdowns in Churachandpur and Kangpokpi as authorities vowed to bring perpetrators to justice amid widening communal tensions.

articles.headline: CBSE Class 12 results: Pass percentage drops to 85.2%
articles.summary: The CBSE declared Class 12 results with an overall pass percentage of 85.2%, the lowest since 2019. Girls outperformed boys with 88.86% against 82.13%. The decline follows the introduction of the Online Screen Marking system. Thiruvananthapuram recorded the highest pass rate at 95.62%, while Prayagraj was the lowest at 72.43%. Over 94,000 students scored above 90%.

articles.headline: Karnataka government revokes ban on hijab in educational institutions
articles.summary: The Karnataka government issued guidelines allowing students to wear faith-based attire, including hijabs and turbans, alongside prescribed uniforms. The order revokes a 2022 ban by the previous BJP government. The new directive emphasizes that educational spaces should promote equality and dignity while clarifying that religious symbols must be supplementary to the uniform.

articles.headline: CBI Director Praveen Sood gets one-year tenure extension
articles.summary: The Centre extended CBI Director Praveen Sood's tenure by one year beyond May 24. The decision was made by a PM-led selection committee despite a dissent note from LoP Rahul Gandhi regarding the selection process. Sood is credited with reducing case pendency from 1,695 in 2020 to 1,048 in 2025. This marks his second extension under amended Delhi Special Police Establishment Act rules.

articles.headline: TN revokes astrologer's appointment as CM's officer on special duty
articles.summary: The Tamil Nadu government rescinded the appointment of astrologer Rickey Radhan Pandit Vettrivel as OSD to CM Vijay within 24 hours of the announcement. The move followed sharp criticism from allies and opponents. Government officials clarified Vettrivel was a long-standing media spokesperson, but the order was withdrawn amid backlash regarding the nature of the post.

articles.headline: Congress to announce Kerala CM choice on Thursday
articles.summary: Ten days after winning a majority in Kerala, the Congress is expected to name its chief ministerial pick on Thursday. Contenders include VD Satheesan, Ramesh Chennithala, and KC Venugopal. Party leadership has completed discussions after observers consulted with state legislators. A meeting of the Congress legislature party in Thiruvananthapuram will finalize the decision.

articles.headline: West Bengal CM orders demolition of illegal Tiljala factory
articles.summary: West Bengal CM Suvendu Adhikari ordered the demolition of an unauthorized five-storey building in Kolkata's Tiljala after a fire killed two people. Power and water connections to illegal buildings in Kasba and Iqbalpur will be cut. Civic authorities deployed bulldozers within 24 hours of the order, and the factory owners have been arrested for violating safety and building norms.

articles.headline: Fire NOCs to be mandatory for all Delhi buildings
articles.summary: Delhi Fire Services is proposing to make fire safety clearances mandatory for all buildings in the national capital, lowering the current height threshold of 17.5 metres. Presently, 95% of structures lack fire NOCs. The proposal aims to expand regulatory oversight and improve monitoring of unsafe older structures to prevent tragedies like recent industrial fires.

articles.headline: Rupee hits new low of 95.805 against US dollar
articles.summary: The Indian rupee fell to a fresh record low of 95.805 against the US dollar on Wednesday. The decline is driven by persistent portfolio capital outflows and rising global oil prices due to the West Asia crisis. Despite India's foreign exchange reserves of over $690 billion, the currency remains under pressure as investors bet on a stronger dollar amid global geopolitical uncertainty.

articles.headline: Punjab Governor slams state law and order situation
articles.summary: Governor Gulab Chand Kataria criticized the Punjab government, citing daily occurrences of contract killings, gang wars, and extortion. In an interview, he noted an 'atmosphere of fear' in the state. While lauding the anti-drug campaign that saw 63,000 arrests, he urged the state to consider anti-conversion laws and expressed concerns over drone-based narcotics smuggling from Pakistan.

articles.headline: France confines 1,700 on cruise ship after passenger death
articles.summary: French authorities confined over 1,700 people aboard the cruise ship 'Ambition' in Bordeaux after a 92-year-old passenger died and 50 others reported gastrointestinal symptoms. While norovirus was ruled out, officials are conducting secondary tests for food poisoning and hantavirus. The death is currently attributed to a heart attack, and no link to a Dutch ship outbreak is established.

articles.headline: Trainee pilot safe after forced landing near Baramati
articles.summary: A trainer aircraft belonging to Redbird Aviation made a forced landing near the Baramati airstrip in Pune after a technical snag. The trainee pilot sustained minor injuries. The DGCA has launched a probe into the incident involving the Tecnam 2008JC aircraft. This follows a high-profile crash in the same area months ago that killed several people, including a deputy CM.

articles.headline: Jaishankar and Lavrov discuss strategic ties ahead of Brics meet
articles.summary: External Affairs Minister S Jaishankar met Russian counterpart Sergey Lavrov in New Delhi to discuss the bilateral strategic partnership. Talks covered trade, investment, energy, and connectivity. The meeting occurred ahead of the Brics foreign ministers' meeting. Jaishankar emphasized that political cooperation with Russia has become more valuable amid a volatile global environment.

articles.headline: Russia’s oil output drops as Ukraine targets energy infrastructure
articles.summary: Russia's crude output fell to 8.8 million barrels per day in April, its sharpest recent reversal, due to Ukrainian drone attacks on Black Sea refineries. While crude exports to new buyers like Egypt rose, oil product exports fell to the lowest level since 2022. Markets are expected to remain undersupplied through October as the West Asia conflict further disrupts global supply chains.

articles.headline: Karnataka to regularise 2.3 million plots in Bengaluru
articles.summary: Deputy CM DK Shivakumar announced the 'Bhoo Guarantee' campaign to resolve khata-related issues for 2.3 million property owners in Bengaluru. The 100-day drive will allow B-Khata to A-Khata conversion at a discounted 2% fee and regularize building violations up to 15%. Special camps will be held every Saturday to help citizens secure property rights ahead of local body elections.

articles.headline: Gelatin sticks found near PM's route were old: Police
articles.summary: Bengaluru police investigators believe gelatin sticks found near PM Modi's convoy route were discarded material rather than an active threat. The explosives appeared to be over two years old and had lost strength. A suspect from Koramangala was arrested after making a hoax bomb call. Authorities are examining travel histories and links to stone quarries in neighboring states.

articles.headline: Kannada actor and producer Dileep Raj dies at 47
articles.summary: Popular Kannada actor and television producer Dileep Raj passed away on Wednesday morning following a heart attack. He was 47. Known for his roles in films like 'Milana' and 'Love Mocktail 3', Raj was a prominent figure in the industry for over two decades. He is survived by his wife and two children. Tributes poured in from the film fraternity for the versatile artist.

articles.headline: SC stays HC order restraining TVK MLA from assembly
articles.summary: The Supreme Court stayed a Madras High Court order that barred TVK MLA Sreenivasa Sethupathi from participating in assembly proceedings. The court termed the High Court’s intervention in the election dispute as 'atrocious' and legally unsustainable, noting that election petitions are the only valid remedy. Sethupathi had won the Thiruppathur seat by a single vote margin.

articles.headline: Bandi Sanjay’s son skips summons in sexual harassment case
articles.summary: Bhageerath, son of Union Minister Bandi Sanjay Kumar, failed to appear before Hyderabad police on Wednesday in a POCSO case. The case involves allegations of sexual harassment of a minor. Police served notice to his family after he could not be located at his residence. Search teams are looking for him across multiple locations as the investigation continues.

articles.headline: Sabarimala: Majoritarianism cannot trump constitutionalism, says SC
articles.summary: A nine-judge Supreme Court bench hearing the Sabarimala reference stated that majoritarian rule cannot override constitutional principles. CJI Surya Kant noted that courts are duty-bound to test all religious practices against constitutional morality. The bench discussed the limits of judicial scrutiny in theological matters while considering individual liberty and gender equality.

articles.headline: NEET shift to computer-based testing faces single-shift hurdle
articles.summary: The transition of NEET-UG to computer-based testing (CBT) remains stalled as the health ministry insists on a single-shift exam for 2.3 million candidates. NTA officials stated that managing such a large volume in one shift is impossible due to infrastructure limits, requiring at least 20 shifts. The move to CBT was recommended by a reform panel to prevent paper leaks.

articles.headline: SC: Law college hostels must not be mere boarding facilities
articles.summary: The Supreme Court issued notice on a plea challenging a Delhi HC ruling that allowed law students to skip classes due to attendance shortages. The bench observed that hostels should not become mere 'boarding and lodging facilities' where academic attendance is ignored. The case emphasizes the importance of classroom learning for professional legal education.

articles.headline: Low-grade explosive device found in Pune hospital washroom
articles.summary: A device containing low-grade explosives, a clock, and a timer was discovered in a washroom at Usha Kiran Hospital in Pune. The Bomb Detection and Disposal Squad neutralized the object at a nearby ground. Police and Anti-Terrorism Squad are investigating all angles, including potential terror links, though no injuries were reported in the incident.

articles.headline: Mulayam Singh Yadav’s stepson Prateek Yadav dies at 38
articles.summary: Prateek Yadav, husband of BJP leader Aparna Yadav and stepson of late SP patriarch Mulayam Singh Yadav, died in Lucknow due to cardiorespiratory collapse caused by a pulmonary thromboembolism. Yadav, 38, had a history of hypertension and recurrant cardiac issues. UP CM Yogi Adityanath and stepbrother Akhilesh Yadav paid tributes to the family at their residence.

articles.headline: Chandra Prakash Goyal named new chairman of CEC
articles.summary: The Union environment ministry reconstituted the Central Empowered Committee (CEC), elevating Chandra Prakash Goyal as its chairman. Goyal is a former director general of forests. The panel, which monitors the implementation of Supreme Court orders on environmental issues, also includes new members Anjan Kumar Mohanty and SC Garkoti.

articles.headline: Rangaswamy takes oath as Puducherry Chief Minister
articles.summary: N Rangaswamy was sworn in as the Chief Minister of Puducherry by Lieutenant Governor K Kailashnathan on Wednesday. Prime Minister Narendra Modi congratulated Rangaswamy, highlighting his record as an effective administrator. The ceremony marks the start of his new term leading the Union Territory's administration.

articles.headline: Suvendu Adhikari takes oath, vacates Nandigram for Bhabanipur
articles.summary: West Bengal CM Suvendu Adhikari took oath as the MLA from Bhabanipur and announced he would vacate his traditional Nandigram seat. Adhikari, who defeated Mamata Banerjee in the polls, promised to keep all campaign commitments to Nandigram voters. He thanked the electorate after the BJP won 207 seats in the 294-member assembly.

articles.headline: India and UAE to sign key energy and LPG pacts
articles.summary: During PM Modi's upcoming visit to the UAE, both nations are expected to sign strategic agreements on LPG supplies and petroleum reserves. The UAE is India’s second-largest destination for petroleum exports and a critical partner for energy security amid West Asia disruptions. Discussions will also focus on doubling bilateral trade to $200 billion by 2032.

articles.headline: NDA states trim convoys and limit trips after PM's appeal
articles.summary: Following PM Modi's call for fuel conservation, CMs of Rajasthan, Assam, Bihar, and Chhattisgarh ordered reductions in official convoys by up to 50%. Measures include limiting foreign travel for officials, promoting public transport, and encouraging work-from-home. The SPG has also reduced the size of the PM's own convoy during recent domestic visits.

articles.headline: Iran welcomes any Indian initiative to end West Asia war
articles.summary: Iranian deputy foreign minister Kazem Gharibabadi stated Tehran would welcome any Indian diplomatic initiative to end the regional war and ensure stability. He expressed hope for a successful Brics summit while criticizing a member state for blocking a joint declaration. Iran also confirmed it is finalizing protocols for vessel fees in the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.headline: Severe storm and rains kill 33 people in Uttar Pradesh
articles.summary: A powerful storm accompanied by heavy rains killed at least 33 people across five districts in Uttar Pradesh, including Bhadohi and Fatehpur. Deaths were caused by wall collapses, uprooted trees, and lightning strikes. CM Yogi Adityanath directed officials to complete relief work within 24 hours and provide immediate compensation to the affected families.

articles.headline: LPG carrier transits Strait of Hormuz bound for Kandla
articles.summary: The Marshall Islands-flagged LPG carrier 'SYMI' successfully transited the Strait of Hormuz and is heading to Kandla port in Gujarat with 20,000 MT of propane and butane. A second vessel is also in transit. The deliveries are crucial for India's domestic supply as US blockades of Iranian ports continue to curb regional crude exports.

articles.headline: Turkey and Qatar may join Pakistan-Saudi defence pact
articles.summary: Pakistan indicated that Turkey and Qatar might join its mutual defence cooperation pact with Saudi Arabia. The move aims to build a broader security platform among 'like-minded' states as the US-Iran war reshapes regional alliances. Pakistan has already deployed fighter jets to Saudi Arabia following strikes on the kingdom’s energy infrastructure.

articles.headline: Russia launches massive drone attack on western Ukraine
articles.summary: Russian forces launched a major daytime drone attack targeting critical infrastructure in western Ukraine, killing six people. The scale of the attack prompted NATO member Poland to scramble fighter jets. The strike highlights the continuing intensity of the conflict as Russia focuses on disrupting energy and logistical hubs deep inside Ukrainian territory.

articles.headline: Report: Covid-19 caused 22.1 million excess deaths globally
articles.summary: The latest World Health Statistical Report estimates 22.1 million excess deaths occurred between 2020 and 2023, triple the documented figure. The pandemic wiped out nearly a decade of gains in life expectancy. The report also warned of stalling progress in universal health coverage and maternal health, with 1.6 billion people pushed into poverty by health expenses.

articles.headline: IT ministry reviews cyber risks from Anthropic's Mythos AI
articles.summary: The IT ministry has formed a team to assess security risks linked to Anthropic's unreleased Mythos AI model. Concerns center on how advanced AI could be used for cyberattacks and phishing. Officials are meeting with Indian firms to emphasize basic cyber hygiene as defensive measures against AI-driven threats that could jeopardize financial and personal data.

articles.headline: Railways to sell shares in IRCTC and Ircon via OFS
articles.summary: The Railway Ministry plans to dilute 5-10% stakes in six listed PSUs, including IRCTC and RITES, to meet a ₹2.62 trillion monetization target. The sales will be conducted via offer-for-sale transactions in FY27. The government aims to unlock value while retaining majority control, with current market prices suggesting potential realizations of up to ₹20,000 crore.

articles.headline: IBM study: AI could add $500bn to India’s economy by 2030
articles.summary: A joint study by IBM and IndiaAI projected that AI could contribute over $500 billion to India’s GDP by 2030. However, only 15% of Indian organizations have successfully scaled AI beyond pilot projects. The report highlighted a shortage of AI skills and infrastructure as major barriers, estimating India will need 350 million AI-literate workers to reach its potential.

articles.headline: Virat Kohli’s century leads RCB to victory over KKR
articles.summary: Virat Kohli scored an unbeaten 105 to guide Royal Challengers Bengaluru to a six-wicket win over Kolkata Knight Riders. Kohli’s effortless hundred, supported by Devdutt Padikkal’s 39, helped RCB chase down a target of 193 in 19 overs. Earlier, Angkrish Raghuvanshi’s 71 had powered KKR to a competitive total. The win keeps RCB at the top of the IPL points table.

articles.headline: PR Sreejesh slams Hockey India after removal as junior coach
articles.summary: Olympic legend PR Sreejesh criticized Hockey India after his contract as junior men's coach was not renewed despite five podium finishes. Sreejesh alleged he was removed to make way for a foreign coach on senior coach Craig Fulton's recommendation. Hockey India stated Sreejesh was offered alternative roles including 'India A' coach and senior goalkeeping coach, which he declined.

articles.headline: Neeraj Chopra to train in Switzerland for Asian Games
articles.summary: Olympic champion Neeraj Chopra will move his training base to Bienne, Switzerland for a 47-day camp as he prepares for the Asian and Commonwealth Games. Chopra is currently recovering from a back injury in Turkiye. The Mission Olympic Cell approved his proposal for foreign training, covering travel and accommodation costs for him and his support staff.

articles.headline: Pune Porsche crash: Doctors arrested for swapping teen's blood samples
articles.summary: Investigation into the Pune Porsche accident reveals the minor driver's blood samples were swapped with another person's to hide alcohol consumption. Two doctors from Sassoon General Hospital and a staff member were arrested for tampering with evidence. Police are also investigating the role of the teen's father and grandfather in allegedly coercing the family driver to take blame.

articles.headline: Delhi hits record 47.4 degrees Celsius as severe heatwave alert continues
articles.summary: Delhi recorded its highest temperature of the season at 47.4 degrees Celsius in Najafgarh. The IMD issued a red alert for the capital, citing a high probability of heat illness for all ages. Residents are advised to avoid outdoor exposure during peak hours. Water shortages have been reported across several parts of the NCR as demand spikes during the extreme weather conditions.

articles.headline: PM Modi targets INDIA bloc over religion-based reservation in Bihar rallies
articles.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi addressed rallies in Bihar, accusing the INDIA bloc of planning to provide reservations based on religion. Modi alleged the opposition wants to snatch quotas from SC, ST, and OBC communities to satisfy their 'vote bank.' He reiterated that the NDA government would protect constitutional rights and prevent any changes to the reservation structure.

articles.headline: ECI warns parties against using children in election campaigning and rallies
articles.summary: The Election Commission of India (ECI) issued a strict directive to political parties to refrain from using children in election-related activities. The commission warned of action against candidates violating these guidelines following complaints of minors present in rallies. The ECI emphasized a zero-tolerance policy toward the exploitation of children for political promotion.

articles.headline: Supreme Court reserves order in Patanjali misleading advertisements contempt case
articles.summary: The Supreme Court reserved its order in the contempt case against Patanjali Ayurved, Baba Ramdev, and Acharya Balkrishna over misleading ads. While the court noted apologies were tendered, it is evaluating the company’s commitment to compliance. The bench also scrutinized the role of state licensing authorities in failing to act against illegal advertisements for herbal medicines.

articles.headline: Bibhav Kumar sent to judicial custody in Swati Maliwal assault case
articles.summary: A Delhi court sent Bibhav Kumar, aide to CM Arvind Kejriwal, to four-day judicial custody regarding the alleged assault on AAP MP Swati Maliwal. Police conducted a forensic reconstruction of the incident at the CM’s residence. Maliwal has accused Kumar of physical assault, while the AAP claims the case is a political conspiracy intended to influence the ongoing general elections.

articles.headline: UGC permits four-year undergraduate degree holders to directly appear for NET
articles.summary: The University Grants Commission (UGC) announced that students with four-year undergraduate degrees can now directly appear for the National Eligibility Test (NET) and pursue a PhD. This removes the previous requirement for a Master's degree. Candidates must have at least 75% marks in their four-year program. The move aims to streamline entry into research under the new education policy.

articles.headline: Thousands attend funeral for Iranian President Raisi and Foreign Minister Abdollahian
articles.summary: Massive crowds gathered in Tabriz and Tehran for the funeral of President Ebrahim Raisi and Foreign Minister Hossein Amir-Abdollahian, who died in a helicopter crash. Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei led the prayers. Iran has announced five days of mourning. Early presidential elections have been scheduled for June 28 to find a successor for the deceased leader.

articles.headline: Spain, Ireland, and Norway announce formal recognition of a Palestinian state
articles.summary: Spain, Ireland, and Norway jointly announced their decision to formally recognize a Palestinian state to accelerate a two-state solution. The move was hailed by Palestinian leaders but sparked a diplomatic crisis with Israel. Israel immediately recalled its ambassadors from the three nations, labeling the decision as a reward for terrorism following the October 7 attacks.

articles.headline: One dead as Singapore Airlines flight hits extreme mid-air turbulence
articles.summary: A Singapore Airlines flight from London made an emergency landing in Bangkok after hitting severe turbulence over the Irrawaddy Basin. A 73-year-old British man died of a suspected heart attack, and over 30 others were injured. The airline’s CEO issued a public apology. Aviation experts and authorities are investigating the incident to determine the severity of the clear-air turbulence.

articles.headline: Israel condemns ICC prosecutor's request for warrants against Netanyahu and Gallant
articles.summary: Israeli PM Benjamin Netanyahu condemned the ICC prosecutor’s request for arrest warrants against him and his defense minister for alleged war crimes in Gaza. Israel and the US challenged the court's jurisdiction, arguing it creates a false equivalence between Israeli leaders and Hamas. The ICC pre-trial chamber must now decide if the evidence warrants the issuance of the orders.

articles.headline: SEBI approves tighter norms for regulated entities associating with 'finfluencers'
articles.summary: The Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI) approved a framework to prevent regulated entities from associating with unregistered financial influencers. The move targets the spread of unauthorized or biased investment advice on social media. Regulated firms must now ensure their partners are SEBI-registered, aiming to protect retail investors from potential financial fraud.

articles.headline: North-bound stretch of Mumbai Coastal Road to open by June 10
articles.summary: The Brihanmumbai Municipal Corporation announced that the north-bound stretch of the Mumbai Coastal Road will open for traffic by June 10. The 10.58-km stretch connects Marine Drive to Worli. While the south-bound arm is already operational, finishing works on the remaining portion are being fast-tracked to ensure completion before the onset of the monsoon season.

articles.headline: Rupee Closes at New Lifetime Low Against US Dollar
articles.summary: The Indian rupee hit a fresh record low of 95.80 against the US dollar on Wednesday, ending at 95.70. The decline was driven by rising crude oil prices and continued outflows from the domestic equity market. Crude oil prices remained above the $107 per barrel mark, putting pressure on emerging market currencies despite intermittent RBI intervention to stem the currency's decline.

articles.headline: RBI Likely to Maintain Status Quo on Interest Rates
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India is expected to maintain current interest rates in its June policy meeting. While three out of 12 economists surveyed suggest a possible rate hike due to persistent inflation and global oil price volatility, the majority expect a pause. Concerns remain regarding elevated food prices and the impact of the West Asia conflict on domestic fiscal targets.

articles.headline: India Plans 2,000 km Subsea Gas Pipeline from Oman
articles.summary: India is accelerating plans for a ₹40,000 crore subsea pipeline to transport natural gas from Oman. The 2,000 km project will run through the Arabian Sea at depths of 3,450 meters to bypass the Strait of Hormuz. The initiative aims to secure India’s energy supply against geopolitical shocks. GAIL and Indian Oil are expected to conduct a feasibility study for the project.

articles.headline: India Becomes Largest Global User Base for Meta AI
articles.summary: India has emerged as the largest user base for Meta AI’s chatbot on WhatsApp, according to Global Chief Will Cathcart. The company reported a significant surge in chat frequency since the launch of the Muse Spark model. Meta is also developing an 'incognito mode' for privacy and exploring replacing physical addresses with geocoded usernames to streamline deliveries.

articles.headline: BJP Leaders Downsize Security Convoys to Reduce Traffic Impact
articles.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi and several BJP Chief Ministers have decided to downsize their security convoys to minimize public inconvenience and fuel consumption. Union Minister Amit Shah and Defence Minister Rajnath Singh have also reduced their vehicle counts. The move aims to set an example for fuel conservation and reduce the impact of VIP movement on city traffic.

articles.headline: Aditya Birla Group and KKR Finalists for Sprng Energy
articles.summary: The Aditya Birla Group and KKR are the final two bidders in a race to acquire Sprng Energy from Shell. The deal is estimated to have an enterprise value of approximately $2 billion. Sprng Energy has a renewable energy portfolio of 5 GW. Shell is selling the asset as part of a strategic review to focus more on core business exploration and production activities.

articles.headline: Militants Kill Three in Manipur’s Kangpokpi District
articles.summary: Three Kuki students were killed and several others injured when unidentified militants opened fire on two vehicles in Manipur’s Kangpokpi district. The victims were returning from a church meeting when they were ambushed on Tiger Road. Local student organizations have called for an immediate investigation and security reinforcements in the hilly region to prevent further violence.

articles.headline: Education Ministry Debates CBT vs Pen-Paper Format for NEET
articles.summary: The Union Education Ministry and the National Testing Agency are debating whether the NEET-UG examination should shift from pen-and-paper to Computer-Based Testing (CBT). The proposal follows the 2024 paper leak controversy. While CBT offers enhanced security, concerns remain regarding the feasibility of conducting tests for 2.2 million students across rural areas in a single day.

articles.headline: India and Russia Review Energy Ties Amid Regional Conflict
articles.summary: External Affairs Minister S Jaishankar and Russian Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov met to review bilateral ties, focusing on energy and economic cooperation amid global tensions. The meeting addressed the ongoing West Asia conflict and ensured the steady supply of Russian oil to India. Jaishankar noted the relationship remains resilient despite external pressures from Western sanctions.

articles.headline: Karnataka Overturns Hijab Ban in Schools and Colleges
articles.summary: The Karnataka government has officially overturned the previous administration's ban on wearing the hijab in educational institutions. The new order states that religious symbols, including turbans and hijabs, are permitted provided they do not interfere with discipline. The decision follows years of legal battles and protests, restoring the right to wear religious attire in classrooms.

articles.headline: Auto Industry Capex to Hit Record ₹40,000 Crore
articles.summary: Leading Indian automakers, including Maruti Suzuki, Hyundai, and Tata Motors, are planning a combined capital expenditure of ₹40,000 crore for the current fiscal year. The investment is driven by strong domestic demand, particularly for SUVs and electric vehicles. Manufacturers are also expanding production capacity to meet long-term growth targets despite global economic headwinds.

articles.headline: Government Urges Manufacturers to Ramp Up Induction Cooktop Production
articles.summary: The government has asked appliance makers to produce millions of induction cooktops over the next 18 months to accelerate the shift away from LPG. The National Efficient Cooking Programme (NECP) aims to distribute 200,000 units initially through bulk procurement. The move is intended to reduce energy import costs and utilize the growing domestic renewable energy capacity.

articles.headline: Airtel Q4 Net Profit Jumps 39% on Strong User Growth
articles.summary: Bharti Airtel reported a 38.7% year-on-year increase in consolidated net profit to ₹7,245 crore for the fourth quarter. The growth was driven by record subscriber additions and increased data consumption, with average revenue per user (ARPU) rising to ₹257. The company's mobile subscriber base in India surpassed 450 million, supported by the rapid expansion of 5G services.

articles.headline: SEBI Plans Broader Intraday Borrowing Rules for Mutual Funds
articles.summary: SEBI has proposed allowing mutual funds to use intraday borrowing facilities to manage liquidity mismatches and redemption pressures. The move aims to provide fund managers with greater flexibility in handling sudden outflows without impacting the scheme's net asset value. The regulator has invited public comments on the proposed framework to enhance market stability.

articles.headline: Fertiliser Subsidy Likely to Surpass Budget Estimates by ₹70,000 Crore
articles.summary: India’s fertiliser subsidy bill is projected to reach ₹2.40 lakh crore this fiscal year, exceeding the budget estimate of ₹1.71 lakh crore. The increase is attributed to rising global prices of raw materials like rock phosphate and phosphoric acid. The government has assured that there will be no hike in retail prices for farmers despite the rising fiscal burden.

articles.headline: Trump Campaign Gets Boost from Pro-Trump AI Influencers
articles.summary: Artificial intelligence-generated influencers are increasingly being used to promote Donald Trump’s 2024 presidential campaign. These virtual avatars parrot political slogans and engage with voters on social media platforms. Experts warn that the rise of hyper-realistic AI deepfakes could influence voter turnout and distort public discourse ahead of the upcoming US elections.

articles.headline: India Junior Hockey Coach Sreejesh Claims Unfair Removal
articles.summary: Former India goalkeeper P R Sreejesh has alleged he was unfairly removed as the head coach of the junior national team. Sreejesh claimed his contract was valid until late 2025, but the federation appointed a new coach without due process. Hockey India denied the allegations, stating the coaching position was properly advertised and filled based on merit and international experience.

articles.headline: Iran and Oman plan new service framework for Hormuz transit
articles.summary: Tehran and Muscat are developing a service-and-payment framework for merchant vessels transiting the Strait of Hormuz. Fees will depend on cargo quantum and ship size, covering navigational support and rescue operations. Iranian Deputy Foreign Minister Kazem Gharibabadi stated the waterway would offer free navigation once the US lifts its blockade and releases frozen Iranian assets.

articles.headline: BEE to revise CAFE-3 norms using E25 as base fuel
articles.summary: The Bureau of Energy Efficiency is expected to update draft Corporate Average Fuel Efficiency-3 (CAFE-3) norms by adopting E25 (25% ethanol blend) as the base fuel instead of E20. The change follows geopolitical tensions in West Asia and a government push for faster ethanol blending to reduce crude oil imports. The new standards are scheduled for implementation from April 2027.

articles.headline: South India faces labour shortage as migrant workers stay home
articles.summary: Manufacturing, construction, and services in Kerala, Tamil Nadu, and Karnataka are struggling with worker shortages after labourers from West Bengal and Assam returned home for elections. Nearly 13 million migrants are employed in the South, and many are now hesitant to return. Employers are offering free air tickets and salary hikes to lure skilled workers back to work sites.

articles.headline: RBI Governor signals readiness to look through transitory oil shocks
articles.summary: RBI Governor Sanjay Malhotra stated the central bank would consider incoming data and might ignore temporary oil price spikes. However, he warned that policy action would be necessary if supply shocks from the West Asia conflict become entrenched. He noted that fiscal coordination is critical and indicated that fuel pump price increases may be considered if global crude costs stay high.

articles.headline: Indian rupee hits fresh low of 95.71 against US dollar
articles.summary: The rupee hit a record closing low of 95.71 against the dollar on Wednesday due to persistent hedging demand from importers. The depreciation occurred despite support from higher customs duties on gold. Traders noted that RBI intervention helped curb volatility, but elevated crude oil prices and continued foreign fund outflows kept the domestic currency under pressure.

articles.headline: Larsen & Toubro secures extra-high voltage substation orders in West Asia
articles.summary: L&T's Power Transmission & Distribution business has secured major engineering, procurement, and construction orders for extra-high voltage substations in West Asia. The stock closed 1.6% higher following the announcement. The projects strengthen the firm's presence in the regional infrastructure market amid ongoing energy expansion projects in the Gulf countries.

articles.headline: TVS Motor reports 17.5% rise in Q4 net profit
articles.summary: TVS Motor Company reported a consolidated net profit of ₹819.55 crore for the quarter ended March 2026, up 17.5% from ₹697.51 crore a year ago. Revenue from operations grew 32% to ₹15,052.73 crore. Two-wheeler and three-wheeler sales rose 28%, while electric vehicle sales surged 51% to 115,000 units during the quarter.

articles.headline: Cipla net profit plunges 55% amid higher R&D and US drag
articles.summary: India's third-largest drugmaker Cipla reported a 54.6% year-on-year drop in Q4 net profit to ₹554.6 crore. The decline was caused by increased research and development spending and higher operating costs at its US manufacturing facilities. Revenue also fell 2.8% to ₹6,541.2 crore. Despite the quarterly drop, the firm recorded its highest-ever annual revenue at ₹28,163 crore.

articles.headline: Delhi High Court orders Google and Apple to remove smut apps
articles.summary: The Delhi High Court directed Google and Apple to take stringent measures against mobile applications hosting pornographic content and facilitating prostitution. A Division Bench stated that intermediaries must exercise due diligence when permitting apps to be uploaded. The court demanded action-taken reports by July following a PIL alleging illegal activities on the app stores.

articles.headline: Passenger vehicle sales in India likely to reach record 5.9 million
articles.summary: Crisil Ratings projects domestic passenger vehicle sales will hit a record 5.9 million units in FY27, representing a 5-7% growth. The surge is driven by utility vehicle demand and previous tax reductions. Utility vehicles are expected to account for 69% of total sales. However, automakers face margin pressure from rising commodity prices and stricter fuel-efficiency regulations.

articles.headline: Jewellery demand pauses across India following sharp duty hike
articles.summary: Jewellery stores in major hubs like Zaveri Bazaar and Dariba Kalan reported empty showrooms and a 'sudden pause' in consumer demand. Bullion prices rocketed overnight after the government increased import duties to 15%. Many customers are postponing purchases or choosing to recycle old ornaments. Artisans and MSME jewellers are seeking survival packages from the government.

articles.headline: Duty hike likely to divert gold and silver imports to UAE route
articles.summary: Higher customs duties on gold and silver are expected to drive more imports from the UAE through the free trade agreement route. Under the India-UAE CEPA, silver imports enjoy a concessional 7% tariff compared to the new 15% general rate. This 8% arbitrage could encourage greater routing of global bullion through Dubai, potentially increasing India's trade deficit with the UAE.

articles.headline: Piyush Goyal sets $1 trillion export target for FY27
articles.summary: Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal has urged stakeholders to target $1 trillion in goods and services exports for the 2026-27 fiscal year. In FY26, exports reached a record $863.11 billion despite global uncertainties. The minister emphasized that ongoing free trade agreements with partners like the UAE, Australia, and the UK are providing preferential market access to Indian businesses.

articles.headline: Tamil Nadu, UP, Mizoram named ‘exemplars’ in logistics performance
articles.summary: Tamil Nadu, Uttar Pradesh, Mizoram, and Delhi have been categorised as top performers in the Commerce Ministry's LEADS 2025 logistics ranking. The index measures the efficiency of logistical services needed to promote exports. These states showed sustained excellence across policy, infrastructure, and regulatory dimensions, contributing to overall national economic growth.

articles.headline: CERC proposes capacity-based market reforms for Indian electricity
articles.summary: The Central Electricity Regulatory Commission (CERC) has issued a paper proposing a capacity market to ensure power system adequacy. The move seeks to address gaps where the energy-only market fails to attract sufficient investment. It aims to guarantee power availability during hours of stress by contracting capacity separately and incentivizing resource adequacy through payments.

articles.headline: EPFO to replace routine inspections with risk-based digital scrutiny
articles.summary: The Employees’ Provident Fund Organisation (EPFO) will introduce a technology-driven compliance framework to replace subjective inspections. Using data analysis and risk profiling, units will be selected for inspection only after repeated non-compliance. The move aims to remove unnecessary human interface and facilitate easier compliance for establishments through a web-based system.

articles.headline: Insurance firms must focus on protection over Ulips: SBI MD
articles.summary: State Bank of India MD Ashwini Tewari stated that insurance companies should prioritize selling protection plans over unit-linked insurance plans (Ulips). He noted that protection products currently account for only 10% of the industry share. Tewari emphasized the responsibility of distributors to ensure customers understand risk factors and avoid overselling investment-heavy products.

articles.headline: NEET (UG) qualification rates decline in 24 states and UTs
articles.summary: Following the 2026 paper leak scandal, data shows a downward trend in NEET qualification rates across 24 states. Between 2019 and 2025, participation rose from 1.4 million to 2.2 million. In 2025, Daman and Diu, Chandigarh, and Delhi recorded the highest year-on-year declines in the share of qualifying candidates. General category candidates saw a 64% qualification rate in the latest cycle.

articles.headline: HPCL consolidated net profit jumps 77% to ₹6,065 crore
articles.summary: Hindustan Petroleum Corporation Ltd (HPCL) reported a 77.58% rise in Q4 net profit, aided by subdued crude oil prices and healthy refining margins. Revenue rose 4% to ₹1.23 lakh crore. Refineries achieved a record annual crude throughput of 26.04 million tonnes. The board recommended a final dividend of ₹19.25 per equity share for the financial year.

articles.headline: Shipping insurance costs to drop 25% via Bharat Maritime Pool
articles.summary: The newly launched $1.5 billion sovereign-backed Bharat Maritime Insurance (BMI) Pool is expected to reduce shipping insurance costs by up to 25%. Managed by GIC Re, the pool provides stability for trade during geopolitical turmoil by offering a domestic fallback for Indian shippers. It will support claims up to $100 million before the government's $1.4 billion guarantee applies.

articles.headline: RBI likely to transfer record dividend to Central government
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India is expected to pay its highest-ever dividend to the government, providing a fiscal cushion against challenges from the West Asia crisis. Last year, the RBI transferred ₹2.69 trillion. Improved profitability at public sector banks and higher income from foreign exchange operations are expected to contribute to a surplus exceeding the government's Budget Estimate.

articles.headline: IEA warns global oil market deficit may persist through 2026
articles.summary: The International Energy Agency (IEA) reports that the global oil market will remain in deficit until the final quarter of 2026. Production in Gulf countries has fallen sharply due to regional conflict. While demand may recover if a deal ends the war, supply will be slower to return. Global oil supply dropped by 1.8 million barrels per day in April to 95.1 million bpd.

articles.headline: SoftBank net profit trebles to $12 billion on OpenAI gains
articles.summary: SoftBank Group reported a net profit of 1.83 trillion yen ($11.60 billion) for the January-March quarter, more than tripling year-on-year. The gain was driven by the rising value of its investment in ChatGPT-maker OpenAI. It marked the company's fifth consecutive quarterly profit. SoftBank also expanded its robotics portfolio and reported record annual gains from its Vision Fund.

articles.headline: Ministers and NDA Chief Ministers downsize convoys following PM appeal
articles.summary: In response to PM Modi's call to conserve fuel and foreign exchange, several Union ministers and CMs have reduced their official convoy sizes. Nitin Gadkari cut his cavalcade by 50%, and CMs in Bihar, Gujarat, and Uttar Pradesh implemented similar reductions. NITI Aayog has moved all seminars online, while Bihar approved an EV policy targeting 30% of new vehicle sales by 2030.

articles.headline: Supreme Court: Hinduism is a way of life, rituals not mandatory
articles.summary: A nine-judge Constitution bench of the Supreme Court observed that Hinduism is a 'way of life' and it is not mandatory for a person to visit a temple to prove their faith. Chief Justice Surya Kant remarked that even lighting a lamp inside one's home is enough to prove one's religion. The observation came during a hearing on religious freedom and gender discrimination at places of worship.

articles.headline: EV startups petition PMO for inclusion in auto PLI scheme
articles.summary: Six electric two-wheeler startups, including Ather and River Mobility, have petitioned the PMO for inclusion in the Production-Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme. Currently, the scheme excludes indigenous startups that cannot meet high revenue and investment thresholds. The startups argue the current rules create a non-level playing field favoring large incumbents and global champions.

articles.headline: Battlegrounds Mobile India paying users rise 17% in Q1
articles.summary: South Korean gaming major Krafton reported a 17% year-on-year growth in paying users for Battlegrounds Mobile India (BGMI) during Q1 2026. Global revenue hit $930 million, driven by PC and mobile titles. The growth in India is attributed to low-cost subscriptions, premium cosmetic 'XSuit' offerings, and collaborations with anime franchises and IPL teams like CSK and KKR.

articles.headline: Government raises precious metal import duties to conserve foreign exchange
articles.summary: The Finance Ministry hiked import duties on gold and silver from 6% to 15%, and platinum from 6.4% to 15.4%. The move aims to safeguard macroeconomic stability and moderate non-essential imports amid global uncertainties from the West Asia crisis. Concessional import routes for gold under the India-UAE Comprehensive Economic Partnership Agreement were also tightened to 14%.

articles.headline: Ladakh prepares to host Astro Week 2026 stargazing festival
articles.summary: Ladakh is organizing 'Ladakh Astro Week 2026' from May 20-26 across locations including Hanle, Nubra Valley, Kargil, and Lamayuru. The event aims to position Ladakh as a global astrotourism destination, featuring solar observations, astro-photography, and stargazing sessions. The Hanle Dark Sky Reserve, home to the Indian Astronomical Observatory, serves as the central hub.

articles.headline: Rolls-Royce and HAL inaugurate expanded manufacturing plant in Hosur
articles.summary: International Aerospace Manufacturing Private Ltd (IAMPL), a joint venture between Rolls-Royce and Hindustan Aeronautics Ltd, opened its expanded facility in Hosur, Tamil Nadu. The expansion is intended to strengthen India's advanced aerospace manufacturing capabilities and supply chain. IAMPL produces high-precision components for civil and defense engine programs.

articles.headline: Honasa Consumer appoints new leaders to spearhead category creation
articles.summary: Honasa Consumer Ltd, the parent of Mamaearth, has named Dheeraj Nagpal and Madhur Acharya to lead its next phase of brand building. The executives will focus on identifying and building new consumer brands as the company seeks to expand its portfolio beyond its current offerings. This marks a strategic push for the firm into the next phase of category development.

articles.headline: Petitioner withdraws plea seeking stay on Tata Trusts board meeting
articles.summary: Petitioner Suresh Tulsiram Patilkhede withdrew his plea from the Bombay High Court after strong criticism regarding the basis of his petition. The plea sought to stall the May 16 board meeting of the Sir Ratan Tata Trust. The court observed that the representations cited as grounds for the urgency were actually filed by third parties rather than the petitioner himself.

articles.headline: Tata Motors to absorb input cost surge to protect truck demand
articles.summary: Tata Motors Commercial Vehicles announced it will absorb a significant portion of rising commodity costs instead of passing them to customers. The decision aims to maintain growth momentum despite inflation in steel, aluminium, rubber, and precious metals. The company expects the domestic commercial vehicle industry to grow in single digits during FY27.

articles.headline: TVS Motor gearing up to launch Norton motorcycles in India
articles.summary: TVS Motor Company plans to launch premium motorcycles under the Norton brand in India and global markets starting Q2 FY27. The company is investing ₹3,000 crore for FY27, with ₹2,000 crore dedicated to product development and new models like Atlas and Atlas GT. TVS acquired the British brand in April 2020 to enter the superbike segment above 850cc.

articles.headline: Tube Investments Q4 revenue grows 20% to ₹6,215 crore
articles.summary: Tube Investments of India (TII) reported robust financial results for Q4 FY26, with consolidated revenue rising 20% year-on-year. Performance was driven primarily by subsidiary CG Power and Industrial Solutions. The board approved long-term borrowing of up to ₹350 crore to meet funding requirements for the upcoming fiscal year.

articles.headline: JBM Auto renames OEM division as EV business unit
articles.summary: JBM Auto's board has renamed its OEM division as the 'EV business' to signal its strategic shift toward electric mobility. The EV segment contributed nearly 38% of consolidated revenue in FY25. JBM commands 79% of the electric tarmac bus market and is exploring a potential $500 million fundraise to support its 10,000-unit electric bus order pipeline.

articles.headline: India and Chile review progress on proposed free trade pact
articles.summary: Chilean Foreign Affairs Minister Francisco Perez Mackenna and Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal reviewed negotiations for an early conclusion of a Comprehensive Economic Partnership Agreement. The talks covered trade facilitation, market access, and investment promotion. Both nations are working to strengthen institutional economic engagement and bilateral trade ties.

articles.headline: OnePlus introduces Nord CE6 smartphone series in Indian market
articles.summary: OnePlus launched the Nord CE6 and Nord CE6 Lite in India, focusing on gaming performance and long battery life. The Nord CE6 uses the Snapdragon 7s Gen 4 platform, while the Lite model features a 7000 mAh battery with 45W fast charging. Open sales for the series began in early May, with the standard model starting at ₹27,999.

articles.headline: Google announces Fitbit transition into unified Health app ecosystem
articles.summary: Google is transitioning the Fitbit app into the Google Health app starting May 19 to manage overall wellness. The update introduces Google Health Coach, an AI-powered feature built on Gemini that offers personalized fitness plans and sleep analysis based on user data. The system continuously adapts using real-time health metrics to generate contextual recommendations.

articles.headline: Epson launches 4K home theatre projector EH-LS9000B in India
articles.summary: Epson's new native 4K projector features 3LCD technology and a solid-state laser light source rated for 20,000 hours. Designed for high-end home environments, it supports screens up to 300 inches and operates at noise levels as low as 22 dB in Eco mode. The device includes HDMI with eARC support and is priced at ₹3,69,999.

articles.headline: reMarkable debuts Paper Pure 10.3-inch tablet for digital writing
articles.summary: reMarkable introduced the Paper Pure, a 360g portable paper tablet designed for document annotation and note-taking. It features a third-generation Canvas display with a 21-millisecond ink response time and a battery life of up to three weeks. The device is built with 38% recycled materials and will begin shipping in June for approximately ₹38,160.

articles.headline: Vivo X300 Ultra review: A premium photography-focused flagship phone
articles.summary: The Vivo X300 Ultra features a 200 MP Sony main sensor and a 200 MP telephoto lens meeting ZEISS standards. It includes a 6.82-inch 2K AMOLED display and is powered by the Snapdragon 8 Elite platform. While priced at ₹1,59,999, it offers professional-grade videography and high color accuracy, competing with top flagships from Apple and Samsung.

articles.headline: Editorial: NEET exam leak requires systemic overhaul of testing agency
articles.summary: Following the annulment of the NEET medical entrance exam due to a paper leak, the editorial argues for a complete transition to online testing. It suggests the National Testing Agency (NTA) adopt technologies similar to the JEE exam, involving encrypted question papers. The leak has impacted 23 lakh aspirants and highlights a need for greater scrutiny and accountability for the NTA.

articles.headline: Opinion: Gold ETFs should unlock vaults to reduce import intensity
articles.summary: A proposal suggests allowing Gold ETFs to use exchange-traded futures for up to 95% of their net assets instead of holding physical gold. This could convert physical demand into a financial risk-management solution, reducing the import burden. Currently, 115 tonnes of gold worth ₹1.7 trillion are stored in vaults, offering zero yield while increasing the national import bill.

articles.headline: SBI seeks Supreme Court review on telecom spectrum ownership ruling
articles.summary: State Bank of India is seeking a review of the Supreme Court's ruling that telecom spectrum is a national resource, not an ordinary corporate asset. The ruling prevents the unrestricted transfer of spectrum through insolvency proceedings. Lenders argue this creates uncertainty in recoveries for distressed telecom companies, as cash flows are tied to spectrum access.

articles.headline: Hantavirus outbreak shows no sign of increased transmissibility or severity
articles.summary: Experts from the European Center for Disease Prevention and Control stated that the hantavirus strain behind a recent cruise ship outbreak is virtually identical to previous South American sequences. While contact tracing is underway for repatriation, data indicates the virus is not behaving differently. Public health risk remains low, though patients may be contagious before symptoms.

articles.headline: Easy Trip Planners board approves ₹500 crore rights issue
articles.summary: Travel platform EaseMyTrip plans to raise ₹500 crore through the issuance of fully paid-up equity shares on a rights basis. The number of securities and the issue price will be determined by the rights issue committee later. The company's board approved the move in a regulatory filing on Wednesday.

articles.headline: SEBI proposes intra-day borrowing facility for asset management companies
articles.summary: The market regulator proposed allowing mutual funds to use intra-day borrowing for trade settlements, forex obligations, and cash-flow mismatches. Currently, borrowing is mainly used for redemptions. The move aims to address operational challenges where fund pay-ins are required in the morning but receivables arrive later in the day. Public comments are invited until June 3.

articles.headline: Defence Ministry pushes TReDS implementation to aid MSME payments
articles.summary: The Ministry of Defence is integrating the Trade Receivables Discounting System (TReDS) with the Government Marketplace (GeM) to improve procurement payment cycles. The initiative allows 16,000 MSMEs linked to defense supply chains to convert invoices into cash quickly. Defence Secretary RK Singh set a meeting to iron out issues for a smooth transition to digital trade financing.

articles.headline: AERA rationalises airport charges for upcoming Noida International Airport
articles.summary: The Airports Economic Regulatory Authority deferred 15.02% of the aggregate revenue requirement for Noida airport to moderate initial charges. The user development fee for domestic departures is fixed at ₹490, significantly lower than the ₹653 proposed by the operator. A variable tariff plan for landing and parking was also approved to support airlines during initial traffic build-up.

articles.headline: RBI eases outward remittance rules via non-bank entity tie-ups
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India removed the requirement for non-bank entities to seek prior approval before forming tie-up arrangements with banks for outward remittance services. Authorized dealers must comply with instructions for cross-border non-trade current account transactions using third-party online modes. The move aims to streamline outward fund transfers from India.

articles.headline: Cipla vows to maintain drug supplies despite West Asia conflict
articles.summary: Cipla is taking proactive steps to ensure consistent pharmaceutical supplies as freight and fuel costs rise due to the prolonged war in West Asia. The company reported a 55% drop in Q4 net profit to ₹555 crore, impacted by higher R&D spend and regulatory challenges in Greece. Cipla aims for its US business to reach $1 billion in revenue by the end of FY27.

articles.headline: USDA projects India to export 2 million tonnes of wheat
articles.summary: India is likely to export at least 2 million tonnes of wheat this year following the removal of a May 2023 export ban. The US Department of Agriculture raised its projection due to hefty supplies in India and a 57-year low in US wheat production. Global prices are hovering at a 23-month high of $6.60 a bushel due to drought conditions in Australia, the US, and Canada.

articles.headline: Agrizy launches GenAI-powered platform to accelerate food product development
articles.summary: Agritech startup Agrizy introduced a generative AI formulation platform that can reduce new product development timelines by up to 40%. The system converts product concepts into manufacturing-ready formulations within minutes, incorporating nutritional profiles and regulatory constraints. It aims to unify ingredient ontology and formulation logic into a single intelligence layer.

articles.headline: ITC agritech platform helps 2.5 million farmers detect crop diseases
articles.summary: ITC's 'Crop Doctor' solution, part of its ITCMAARS platform, uses AI-based image analytics to diagnose diseases and pest infestations across 70 crops. The tool has contributed to a 10-15% reduction in fertilizer use and a 25% increase in farm incomes. ITC aims to eventually reach 10 million farmers through the agriculture-focused digital stack.

articles.headline: Central government staff forum seeks work from home guidelines
articles.summary: An association representing Central government employees asked the Centre to issue guidelines for work from home and virtual conferencing. The request follows Prime Minister Narendra Modi's call to cut down avoidable expenditure due to the West Asia crisis. The forum suggests that remote work can help reduce national fuel consumption and travel costs.

articles.headline: PM Narendra Modi opts for smaller convoy for war-linked austerity
articles.summary: Prime Minister Modi was seen traveling through New Delhi in a convoy of just two cars, signaling a shift toward governance minimalism. Union Ministers and Chief Ministers like Yogi Adityanath and Mohan Yadav followed suit by reducing their security fleets. The PM also directed the induction of electric vehicles into VVIP convoys where possible to reduce fuel consumption.

articles.headline: Telangana government plans 600 new EV charging stations by 2029
articles.summary: The Telangana Renewable Energy Development Corporation intends to set up 600 electric vehicle charging and battery swapping stations over three years. The long-term target is to reach 12,000 stations by 2035. The state currently has over 2.8 lakh registered EVs, with 91,572 new registrations in the last fiscal year. Hyderabad currently hosts approximately 600 existing stations.

articles.headline: Air India suspends six international routes amid high fuel costs
articles.summary: Tata Group-backed Air India has temporarily suspended services on routes including Delhi-Chicago, Delhi-Shanghai, and Mumbai-Dhaka through August. The airline cited high jet fuel prices and airspace restrictions. Frequencies to destinations in Australia, Europe, and Southeast Asia have also been reduced to improve network stability during the peak travel season.

articles.headline: FMCG firms cut advertising budgets as demand slows in rural markets
articles.summary: Leading FMCG companies, including Marico, Dabur, and HUL, reduced advertising and promotional spends by up to 10% in the last quarter. Firms are recalibrating strategies to focus on targeted digital and influencer-led campaigns rather than mass media. Analysts attribute the pullback to subdued volume growth and rising input cost pressures hitting discretionary spending.

articles.headline: Supreme Court stays Calcutta High Court order canceling West Bengal OBC certificates
articles.summary: The Supreme Court stayed a Calcutta High Court order that invalidated nearly 5 lakh OBC certificates issued in West Bengal since 2010. The apex court noted the cancellation would disrupt ongoing admissions and recruitments while asking the state to explain its classification basis. The High Court had found the process bypassed the Backward Classes Commission and circumvented reservation laws.

articles.headline: KKR enters IPL 2024 final after dominant eight-wicket win over Sunrisers Hyderabad
articles.summary: Kolkata Knight Riders secured a spot in the IPL 2024 final with an eight-wicket victory over Sunrisers Hyderabad in Qualifier 1 at the Narendra Modi Stadium. Mitchell Starc’s three-wicket haul dismantled SRH, while Venkatesh and Shreyas Iyer hit half-centuries to chase 160 in 13.4 overs. SRH will play Qualifier 2 in Chennai against the Eliminator winner for another chance to reach the final.

articles.headline: Government hikes import duties on gold, silver, and platinum to 15%
articles.summary: The Centre raised import duties on gold and silver from 6% to 15%, and platinum to 15.4%, to curb non-essential imports and preserve foreign exchange reserves. Including a 3% IGST, the total tax incidence on gold and silver rises to 18.45%. PM Modi urged citizens to limit gold purchases amid global energy disruptions. Experts anticipate further tightening of remittance schemes to manage deficits.

articles.headline: CM Joseph Vijay wins Tamil Nadu trust vote as AIADMK faces split
articles.summary: CM C. Joseph Vijay’s TVK government won a confidence motion with 144 votes, aided by 25 rebel AIADMK lawmakers. The DMK walked out alleging horse-trading. Following the vote, AIADMK leader E. Palaniswami removed 26 leaders, including 12 legislators. Vijay, heading a minority government, pledged to focus on social justice and secularism during the TVK-led administration’s term.

articles.headline: Cabinet approves ₹37,500 crore scheme to promote coal and lignite gasification
articles.summary: The Union Cabinet cleared a ₹37,500 crore incentive scheme to gasify up to 100 million tonnes of coal and lignite by 2030. The project aims to produce synthesis gas for chemicals like methanol and ammonia, reducing India's import bill. Financial support covers 20% of plant costs, capped at ₹5,000 crore per project, and is expected to attract investments up to ₹3 trillion across 25 projects.

articles.headline: CBI arrests five across three states in NEET-UG 2026 paper leak case
articles.summary: The CBI arrested five individuals from Jaipur, Gurugram, and Nashik for their involvement in the NEET-UG 2026 paper leak. Suspects include a BAMS graduate and members of a Rajasthan-based family. Investigators traced a financial chain spanning Maharashtra, Haryana, and Rajasthan. Digital devices were seized for forensic examination as the agency probes an organized network in multiple states.

articles.headline: Assam Cabinet approves Uniform Civil Code bill excluding tribal populations
articles.summary: The Himanta Biswa Sarma-led Assam Cabinet approved the Uniform Civil Code (UCC) legislation, set to be tabled on May 26. The bill will regulate marriage, divorce, and inheritance, replacing religion-specific personal laws while exempting the state's tribal population. Assam becomes the first state with a significant Muslim population to move toward standardizing civil laws in this manner.

articles.headline: Donald Trump arrives in Beijing for high-stakes summit with Xi Jinping
articles.summary: US President Donald Trump arrived in Beijing for a two-day summit with Xi Jinping, accompanied by CEOs including Elon Musk and Jensen Huang. Discussions cover trade tariffs, AI technology, and regional security, including Taiwan arms sales and the Iran conflict. While Trump seeks to reduce the trade deficit, Chinese media warned that Taiwan remains a "red line" in bilateral relations.

articles.headline: Amul and Mother Dairy hike milk prices by ₹2 per litre
articles.summary: Major milk cooperatives Amul and Mother Dairy increased fresh pouch milk prices by ₹2 per litre across India, effective May 14. The 2.5% to 3.5% hike, the first since May 2025, is attributed to rising costs of cattle feed, packaging, and fuel. The move aims to ensure remunerative prices for farmers, with procurement rates also rising by ₹30 per kg fat to maintain supply stability.

articles.headline: Air India to cancel 400 international flights monthly through August
articles.summary: Air India will reduce over 120 weekly international flights (approximately 400 per month) from June to August to improve network stability. Impacted routes include Delhi to San Francisco, Paris, and Chicago. The airline cited surging jet fuel prices and West Asia airspace restrictions as key reasons. Affected passengers are being offered full refunds or free rebooking amid industry stress.

articles.headline: Cabinet approves Nagpur airport upgrade and ₹20,667 crore Ahmedabad-Dholera rail project
articles.summary: The Union Cabinet approved the modernization of Nagpur’s International Airport under a PPP model with GMR Nagpur for 30 years, targeting 30 million annual passengers. Additionally, a ₹20,667 crore, 134-km semi-high-speed double rail line was cleared to connect Ahmedabad and Dholera by 2031, benefiting 284 villages and the Dholera Special Investment Region and upcoming airport.

articles.headline: Government hikes MSP for 14 Kharif crops for 2026-27 season
articles.summary: The Union Cabinet increased the Minimum Support Price (MSP) for 14 kharif crops, ensuring margins of 50-61% over production costs. Paddy MSP rose to ₹2,441 per quintal, while sunflower seeds saw the highest hike of ₹622 to ₹8,343. Total payout to farmers is estimated at ₹2.6 lakh crore. Hikes for oilseeds, pulses, and cotton aim to cut imports, while maize saw a nominal ₹10 increase.

articles.headline: Bharti Airtel Q4 net profit drops 33.5% to ₹7,325 crore
articles.summary: Bharti Airtel reported a 33.5% decline in Q4 net profit to ₹7,325 crore, due to a high base from last year's one-time tax credit. Revenue rose nearly 16% to ₹55,383 crore, while ARPU improved to ₹257, leading over Reliance Jio. The telco added 5.8 million smartphone users and saw growth in its home broadband and Africa units. Sunil Mittal was reappointed Chairman for five years.

articles.headline: Sindhu, Srikanth, and Lakshya advance to second round of Thailand Open
articles.summary: PV Sindhu, Lakshya Sen, and Kidambi Srikanth progressed to the Thailand Open Super 500 second round in Bangkok. Sindhu defeated Tung Ciou-Tong and Jaslyn Hooi, while Srikanth upset Loh Kean Yew and Wang Tzu-wei. Lakshya Sen also moved forward after a hard-fought match. Ayush Shetty was the only notable Indian exit, losing to Japan’s Kodai Naraoka. The tournament is a Super 500 event.

articles.headline: Bharti Airtel board approves ₹28,220 crore share-swap deal with ICIL
articles.summary: Bharti Airtel’s board cleared a ₹28,220 crore share-swap deal to increase its stake in Airtel Africa by issuing shares to promoter entity Indian Continent Investment Limited. This transaction increases the Mittal family’s stake in Airtel Africa to 79.04% and ICIL’s holding in Bharti Airtel by 2.3%. The board also reappointed Sunil Bharti Mittal and Nisaba Godrej for five-year terms.

articles.headline: Domestic gold and silver prices surge 7% following import duty hike
articles.summary: Domestic gold and silver prices surged 7% on the MCX after a 15% duty hike. Gold reached a record ₹1,63,165 per 10 grams, and silver hit ₹2,95,805 per kg. Gold and silver ETFs, like Nippon India, rose over 5.7%. While international prices fell, local markets repriced due to the tariff change, which aims to curb imports, narrow the trade deficit, and support the domestic currency.

articles.headline: Tata Motors Q4 profit rises on strong commercial vehicle demand
articles.summary: Tata Motors reported a robust March quarter, with its commercial vehicle (CV) segment net profit jumping 70% to ₹2,406 crore. Consolidated revenue grew 19.4% to ₹26,098 crore, driven by strong demand for heavy trucks and buses. For the full fiscal year, the company achieved its highest-ever revenue and EBITDA margin, maintaining a 35.7% share in the domestic commercial vehicle market.

articles.headline: Eli Lilly to launch Lormalzi Alzheimer's drug in India this month
articles.summary: Eli Lilly and Company will launch Lormalzi (donanemab), a monthly amyloid plaque targeting therapy for Alzheimer’s disease, in India this month. Priced at approximately ₹91,688 per 350 mg vial, the injectable treatment is indicated for patients with mild cognitive impairment or dementia. The company plans an access program as India’s dementia cases are projected to reach 19 million by 2036.

articles.headline: Zydus Lifesciences to acquire US-based Assertio Holdings for ₹1,592 crore
articles.summary: Zydus Lifesciences has agreed to acquire Nasdaq-listed Assertio Holdings in an all-cash deal valued at $166.4 million (approx. ₹1,592 crore). The offer of $23.5 per share represents a 75.8% premium. The acquisition, expected to close in Q2 2026, provides Zydus with a specialty oncology commercial platform in the US, strengthening its portfolio in developed pharmaceutical markets.

articles.headline: Uber and Adani Group partner to build first India data centre
articles.summary: Uber CEO Dara Khosrowshahi announced a partnership with the Adani Group to establish the ride-hailing giant’s first data centre in India. The facility will be used to test and deploy global technology at scale. The move aligns with Adani's ₹5,000 crore commitment for 100 MW renewable-powered data centres. India is currently Uber’s third-largest market, facilitating 1.2 billion annual trips.

articles.headline: SEBI orders former Religare chair Rashmi Saluja to disgorge ₹2 crore
articles.summary: SEBI has ordered former Religare Enterprises Chairperson Rashmi Saluja to disgorge ₹1.99 crore in avoided losses and pay a ₹40 lakh penalty for insider trading. Saluja allegedly sold shares while in possession of unpublished price-sensitive information regarding a 2023 open offer by the Burman Group. The regulator rejected her defense that the sale was intended to fund the exercise of ESOPs.

articles.headline: Federal Bank, Indian Bank and Nalco added to MSCI India Index
articles.summary: Global index provider MSCI has added Federal Bank, Indian Bank, National Aluminium, and Multi Commodity Exchange of India to its Global Standard Index, effective May 29, 2026. Conversely, Hyundai Motor India, Jubilant Foodworks, Kalyan Jewellers, and Rail Vikas Nigam will be removed. Adani Energy Solutions was excluded due to NSE surveillance. The reshuffle will drive significant capital flows.
